meaning: adj.不停的,连续的,未受干扰的,不间断的
examples: We had an uninterrupted view of the stage.
Diners can enjoy an uninterrupted view of the gardens.
We managed to eat our meal uninterrupted by phone calls.
"We are investing now to help supply uninterrupted energy when the global economy recovers, " said al-Naimi.
Liang said he recognized the U.S. desire for military-to-military ties that are uninterrupted by political disagreements.
